name: DMV: Vehicle Registration Renewal, Address Change, or License Replacement
description: Call a state DMV / MVA / BMV / DOL to renew a vehicle registration, change the address on file, or order a replacement driver's license. Most DMVs prefer online self-service — this skill is the fallback when the online flow rejects the user or a hold/flag must be cleared by a human.
disclaimer: Not legal advice. DMV procedures vary by state. The agent will not advise on traffic-court strategy, license-suspension appeals, or DUI consequences — those require an attorney or the state's hearing officer.

context.when_to_use: User cannot complete the task online (system rejects them, a flag/hold appears, an out-of-state transfer is needed, or the renewal window is too tight for mail). Also use when the user has received conflicting information from the website vs. a notice in the mail.
preconditions: User has their driver's-license number OR last 4 of SSN (state-dependent).
User has the license-plate number and current registration card / VIN if renewing a vehicle.
User has the new address (with ZIP+4 if known) for an address change.
User knows whether they need a REAL ID upgrade or a standard renewal.
User has a callback number — DMV phone lines drop frequently.

principles: DMV hold music is the longest in government. Settle in. Do NOT keep redialing — you lose your place.
Front-line agents read from a script. If your case is unusual, politely ask for a 'tier 2' or 'supervisor review' — they have lookup tools the front line does not.
Most DMVs route by ZIP / county. Calling the wrong field office wastes 40 minutes. Confirm you are at the right office before describing the issue.
Get a confirmation / reference number for ANY change. DMV systems are notoriously slow to propagate and the paper trail saves you later.
Recognize 'this can only be done in person' early — for many states, license replacement REQUIRES a field-office visit (REAL ID, photo capture). Don't burn 60 minutes on hold to be told to come in.

phrases.opener_renewal: Hi, I'm trying to renew the registration on my vehicle. My plate is [PLATE] and my renewal notice says it's due [DATE]. The online portal won't let me complete the renewal — it's giving me [ERROR]. Can you help me figure out what's blocking it?
phrases.opener_address: Hi, I need to update the address on my driver's license and vehicle registration. The new address is [ADDRESS]. Can we do both in one call?
phrases.opener_replacement: Hi, I need a replacement driver's license — the original was [lost / stolen / damaged]. Can this be ordered by phone or do I need to come in?
phrases.confirm_right_office: Just to confirm — am I at the office that handles [county/region]? I want to make sure I'm in the right queue.
phrases.ask_for_hold_reason: The system says there's a hold or flag on my record. Can you tell me what it is and what office can clear it?
phrases.stall_documents: Give me one second — I want to pull up the notice in front of me so I read the numbers correctly.
phrases.graceful_close_success: Thank you. Before we hang up: can I have a confirmation number, your name or agent ID, and the expected date I'll see the change reflected online or receive the document in the mail?
phrases.graceful_close_failure: I understand. Just so I know my next step — what's the best office, web page, or form for me to use, and is there a way to schedule an in-person appointment from here?

tactics.name: Verify you reached the correct office FIRST
tactics.when: Within the first 60 seconds of reaching a human.
tactics.script: Confirm the office handles the user's county/region and the task type. If you're at a call center that only handles licensing and the user needs registration, ask for the right transfer number before describing the full case.

tactics.name: Surface holds and flags before the renewal attempt
tactics.when: User's online attempt failed with a generic error.
tactics.script: Ask 'Can you check if there are any holds, flags, or outstanding items on the record?' Common blockers: unpaid toll, emissions/smog requirement, insurance lapse, parking citation, court-ordered suspension. Each routes to a different office.

tactics.name: Bundle related changes
tactics.when: User needs both license + registration address changes.
tactics.script: Most states will update both in one call IF you ask explicitly. Front-line reps default to one at a time.

tactics.name: Capture the document path
tactics.script: Ask: 'Will I get an updated card / sticker in the mail, do I need to print something, or is the change electronic-only?' DMV cards in the mail take 2–6 weeks; some states require the user to print a temporary registration.

tactics.name: Escalate to tier 2 / supervisor for unusual cases
tactics.when: Front-line rep says 'system won't let me' but cannot explain why.
tactics.script: Politely ask for a supervisor or a 'records specialist'. Many DMV systems have override codes that front-line agents cannot use.

boundaries: Do NOT give legal advice on tickets, suspensions, DUIs, or court-ordered restrictions — refer to an attorney or the issuing court.
Do NOT share SSN unless the rep has authenticated themselves and the field is necessary for lookup. Last 4 is usually enough.
Do NOT promise the user the renewal will arrive by a specific date if the rep gave a range — quote the range exactly.
Do NOT attempt to clear a court-ordered hold by phone — those require the originating court.
Do NOT claim REAL ID compliance unless the rep confirms it on this transaction.
success_signals: Rep gives a confirmation / reference number for the change.
Rep confirms an exact mail-out date or document arrival window.
Rep clears or identifies a previously unknown hold.
Rep offers to schedule an in-person appointment if the task can't be done by phone.
failure_signals: Rep insists the task can only be done in person at a field office (note this is NOT necessarily failure — capture the next step).
Rep cannot identify why the online portal rejected the user and refuses to escalate.
Hold time exceeds 60 minutes with no human pickup.
User has been transferred more than twice and is being routed in a loop.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm the change, capture the confirmation number, agent name/ID, expected document arrival, and any temporary document (printable receipt, temp registration). Note the next renewal date in the user's calendar.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Capture the exact next step: which office, which form (DL-XX, REG-XX), in-person required Y/N, appointment URL. Report back to the operator so they can choose between rebooking, in-person visit, or mail-in.
follow_ups: Calendar arrival of the new card/sticker; ping operator if it doesn't show within the quoted window.
If REAL ID was deferred, add a calendar reminder before the federal deadline.
If a hold was identified, dispatch a follow-up call to the office that owns the hold (court, tolling authority, insurance carrier).

required_user_info: Driver's license number OR last 4 of SSN
Current and new address (with ZIP+4 if available)
Reason for replacement (lost / stolen / damaged / name change)
Whether the user wants REAL ID or standard
hang_up_and_call_back_signals: You realize you are at the WRONG office (e.g. licensing call center for a registration issue) — politely end and call the correct number.
You've been on hold > 75 minutes with no movement — try the next morning at opening (call volume drops 30–60% in the first hour).
Rep is reading from a script that does not match the user's situation and refuses to escalate — hang up, call back, and ask for tier 2 by name.
